summary: Hardened the watcher-side merge-agent trigger so it now fails closed on verdict/state gaps, respects operator stop labels and remediation budget, and records only durable successful dispatches.
validation: Rebased the branch onto origin/main with `git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main`.

addressed.finding: The do-not-merge label is not honored and the label set diverges from spec.
addressed.action: Replaced the watcher skip-label set with `merge-agent-skip` plus `do-not-merge`, removing the unsupported `merge-agent-stuck` special case.

addressed.finding: Idempotency is windowed by 10 minutes instead of durable `(repo, pr, headSha)` state.
addressed.action: Dropped the time-window gate and keyed dispatch records by stable repo/PR/SHA so an existing successful launch blocks redispatch until the head SHA changes.

addressed.finding: Verdict parsing exact-matches `Request changes` and can misfire on markdown, casing, or suffix text.
addressed.action: Normalized extracted verdict text, treated `Request changes` variants as blocking, allowed only recognized `Comment only`/`Approved` verdicts through, and fail-closed on unknown verdict strings.

addressed.finding: Missing latest follow-up job / null verdict fails open and still dispatches.
addressed.action: Changed the dispatch picker to return `skip-no-verdict` when no verdict can be recovered instead of treating missing review data as mergeable.

addressed.finding: A dispatch record is written before `hq` succeeds, creating retry dead zones and overwriting the launch record.
addressed.action: Moved durable dispatch recording to the post-launch success path only, switched the ledger to one stable record per repo/PR/SHA, and made non-JSON `hq` output a hard failure instead of silently nulling IDs.

addressed.finding: No PR-state check exists for closed or merged PRs in the watcher-side merge-agent path.
addressed.action: Added explicit open-state guards in both the watcher poll loop and the `gh pr view` candidate fetch so closed or merged PRs short-circuit before dispatch.

addressed.finding: The remediation-round-budget gate from PMO-B2 is missing.
addressed.action: Plumbed the latest follow-up job status and remediation round counters into the merge-agent dispatch decision and now skip dispatch while remediation is still pending, in progress, or still claimable under the stored max-round budget.

pushback.finding: Wrong enforcement layer: move the trigger/ledger into the daemon or amend the Track B spec before landing watcher-side dispatch.
pushback.reasoning: Relocating the feature into the daemon and editing the external PMO-B2 project files is larger than this remediation round and those project-spec files are not present in this checkout, so I hardened the existing watcher-side path instead of attempting an unscoped cross-subsystem rewrite.

reReview.reason: The watcher-side merge-agent path now fails closed on verdict, lifecycle, and remediation-budget gates and is ready for another adversarial review pass.
